{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Pharmacy Assistant",
  "description": "Stretch-it Concepts a human resource outsourcing solutions company, bureau of recruitment, performance management and training and development company situated in Lagos, Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Pharmacy Assistant

Job Location: Ogba, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time (Shifts)
Reports To: Pharmacist

Job Summary


	The Pharmacy Assistant supports the pharmacist in product handling, stock management, and customer support within the pharmacy and works strictly under the supervision of a licensed pharmacist.


Key Responsibilities
Pharmacy Operations Support:


	Assist the pharmacist in organizing and preparing medications (non-dispensing).
	Receive and arrange pharmaceutical products on shelves.
	Ensure medications and health products are properly labeled and stored.
	Assist to pick up products when necessary.


Inventory Management:


	Monitor stock levels and report low or expired items.
	Check expiry dates regularly and remove expired products.
	Assist with stock counts and inventory records.
	Assist in entering products in system when they arrive.


Customer Support:


	Guide customers to appropriate products as directed by the pharmacist.
	Refer all medical advice and prescription - related questions to the pharmacist.
	Provide basic product information for non-prescription items only.


Compliance &amp; Safety:


	Maintain cleanliness and hygiene of pharmacy shelves and work areas.
	Follow regulatory and safety guidelines governing pharmacy operations.
	Maintain confidentiality of patient and prescription information.


Qualifications &amp; Skills


	Minimum of OND certificate.
	At least 2 years of pharmacy experience.
	Good communication skills
	Good organizational skills.
	Ability to follow instructions and work under supervision.
	Proximity to Ogba, Agege, and Ikeja environs is key.
	Attention to detail and respect for safety procedures.


Salary
N95,000 - N120,000 / Month (depends on experience)

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: jobs@stretchitconcepts.com using &quot;Pharmacy Assistant&quot; as the subject of the mail.",

Pharmacy Assistant Job at Stretch-it Concepts




        
    

        
        Stretch-it Concepts a human resource outsourcing solutions company, bureau of recruitment, performance management and training and development company situated in Lagos, Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Pharmacy Assistant

Job Location: Ogba, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time (Shifts)
Reports To: Pharmacist

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Pharmacy Assistant supports the pharmacist in product handling, stock management, and customer support within the pharmacy and works strictly under the supervision of a licensed pharmacist.


Key Responsibilities
Pharmacy Operations Support:


	Assist the pharmacist in organizing and preparing medications (non-dispensing).
	Receive and arrange pharmaceutical products on shelves.
	Ensure medications and health products are properly labeled and stored.
	Assist to pick up products when necessary.


Inventory Management:


	Monitor stock levels and report low or expired items.
	Check expiry dates regularly and remove expired products.
	Assist with stock counts and inventory records.
	Assist in entering products in system when they arrive.


Customer Support:


	Guide customers to appropriate products as directed by the pharmacist.
	Refer all medical advice and prescription - related questions to the pharmacist.
	Provide basic product information for non-prescription items only.


Compliance & Safety:


	Maintain cleanliness and hygiene of pharmacy shelves and work areas.
	Follow regulatory and safety guidelines governing pharmacy operations.
	Maintain confidentiality of patient and prescription information.


Qualifications & Skills


	Minimum of OND certificate.
	At least 2 years of pharmacy experience.
	Good communication skills
	Good organizational skills.
	Ability to follow instructions and work under supervision.
	Proximity to Ogba, Agege, and Ikeja environs is key.
	Attention to detail and respect for safety procedures.


Salary
N95,000 - N120,000 / Month (depends on experience)

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Pharmacy Assistant" as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Recruitment Officer Job at Westfield Consulting Limited
content:
Westfield Consulting Limited - Our client, a Consulting firm in the Human Resources space, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Recruitment Officer

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Consulting (HR)

Job Purpose


	Coordinate recruitment briefs from various clients and ensure that all recruitments are in line with the client’s requirements


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Sourcing and reaching out to qualified candidates for current open roles.
	Serving as a go-between for candidates and clients.
	Co-ordinating the entire hiring process end to end.
	Evaluating candidates for roles of various fields.
	Network with potential hires through professional groups on social media
	Collaborating with clients to set qualification criteria for future employees where the need arises.
	Detailed screening of both resumes and job applications to match the requirements of clients.
	Conduct initial phone screens to create shortlists of qualified candidates.
	Interviewing candidates for a wide range of roles i.e. Junior, Senior and Executive.
	Maintaining a database of candidates for major / frequent / popular job roles.
	Follow up with candidates through the hiring process.
	Development of interview techniques and assessment methods.
	Other duties and responsibilities as assigned from time to time in line with overall job purpose.


Requirements


	First Degree in a numerate or business-related discipline
	Minimum of 2 years of cognate work experience in Human Resources / Business Development
	Experience in Consulting is an added advantage
	Strong networking ability
	Knowledge of company services and the ability to sell them


Salary
Competitive and Negotiable (depending on experience).

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their current CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Cormart Nigeria Limited is a leading specialty chemicals and industrial raw materials company with over 40 years of experience in manufacturing and distribution across Nigeria and West Africa. As part of the TGI Group, Cormart provides premium chemical solutions to industries including construction, paints, food and beverage, cosmetics, pharmaceuticals, and furniture manufacturing.

The company offers a wide range of products such as adhesives, sealants, waterproofing solutions, paint chemicals, and personal care ingredients, supported by a strong distribution network and a people-focused culture that promotes growth, performance, and long-term career development.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Executive

Job Location: Bariga, Lagos

Job Summary&nbsp;


	We manufacture and sell Bond, PVC Glue, Sealants, and Waterproof Powder &mdash; and we need people who know their markets and know how to sell.


Why Cormart?


	Established brand &mdash; part of the TGI Group, 40+ years in Nigeria
	Competitive salary and performance-based commission
	Clear career growth path based on results.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to career.cormart@clicktgi.net using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.",

Sales Executive Job at Cormart Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Cormart Nigeria Limited is a leading specialty chemicals and industrial raw materials company with over 40 years of experience in manufacturing and distribution across Nigeria and West Africa. As part of the TGI Group, Cormart provides premium chemical solutions to industries including construction, paints, food and beverage, cosmetics, pharmaceuticals, and furniture manufacturing.

The company offers a wide range of products such as adhesives, sealants, waterproofing solutions, paint chemicals, and personal care ingredients, supported by a strong distribution network and a people-focused culture that promotes growth, performance, and long-term career development.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Position: Sales Executive

Job Location: Bariga, Lagos

Job Summary 


	We manufacture and sell Bond, PVC Glue, Sealants, and Waterproof Powder — and we need people who know their markets and know how to sell.


Why Cormart?


	Established brand — part of the TGI Group, 40+ years in Nigeria
	Competitive salary and performance-based commission
	Clear career growth path based on results.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Job Position: HSE Officer

Job Locations: Kwall, Plateau; Iseyin, Oyo and Ilorin, Kwara

Summary


	The HSE Officer is responsible for ensuring compliance with health, safety, environmental, and workplace standards across PMN Farms operations while promoting a culture of safety, risk awareness, and regulatory compliance.


Description


	The role develops, implements, monitors, and continuously improves health, safety, and environmental programs designed to protect employees, visitors, company assets, and the environment.


Responsibilities


	Conduct routine safety inspections and audits.
	Identify workplace hazards and recommend corrective actions.
	Develop and implement HSE policies and procedures.
	Conduct risk assessments for operational activities.
	Investigate incidents, accidents, and near misses.
	Prepare incident and safety reports.
	Conduct employee safety training and awareness programs.
	Monitor compliance with environmental regulations.
	Ensure proper use of personal protective equipment (PPE).
	Maintain HSE records and documentation.
	Support emergency preparedness and response activities.
	Monitor biosecurity, sanitation, and workplace safety standards.


Requirements


	Degree or HND in HSE, Environmental Science, Engineering, Occupational Health, or related fields.
	Minimum 2 years of experience in HSE management, safety compliance, or environmental management.
	Strong understanding of occupational health and safety principles.
	Ability to conduct inspections and audits.
	Strong reporting and documentation skills.
	Ability to work independently and enforce compliance standards. Professional
	HSE certifications are an added advantage.


Technical Skills:&nbsp;


	Risk assessment and hazard identification
	Health and safety management
	Incident investigation
	Report writing
	Training and facilitation
	Communication and leadership
	Regulatory compliance.


Remuneration


	Competitive and based on experience.

	Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

HSE Officer Job at Pebbles, Marks & Nana Farms Limited




        
    

        
        Pebbles, Marks & Nana Farms Limited is a Nigerian-owned agricultural company committed to sustainable farming, quality food production, and community development. Incorporated in September 2018 as a private company limited by shares, we operate within Nigeria’s agricultural sector with a focus on responsible crop cultivation and long-term growth.

Rooted in strong values and guided by practical experience, our operations are designed to support food security, create employment, and contribute meaningfully to local and regional agricultural supply chains. Turn your agricultural dreams into thriving realities with expert support from land acquisition to harvest.

We are recruiting to fill position below:

Job Position: HSE Officer
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Locations: Kwall, Plateau; Iseyin, Oyo and Ilorin, Kwara
Work arrangement: Onsite

Summary


	The HSE Officer is responsible for ensuring compliance with health, safety, environmental, and workplace standards across PMN Farms operations while promoting a culture of safety, risk awareness, and regulatory compliance.


Description


	The role develops, implements, monitors, and continuously improves health, safety, and environmental programs designed to protect employees, visitors, company assets, and the environment.


Responsibilities


	Conduct routine safety inspections and audits.
	Identify workplace hazards and recommend corrective actions.
	Develop and implement HSE policies and procedures.
	Conduct risk assessments for operational activities.
	Investigate incidents, accidents, and near misses.
	Prepare incident and safety reports.
	Conduct employee safety training and awareness programs.
	Monitor compliance with environmental regulations.
	Ensure proper use of personal protective equipment (PPE).
	Maintain HSE records and documentation.
	Support emergency preparedness and response activities.
	Monitor biosecurity, sanitation, and workplace safety standards.


Requirements


	Degree or HND in HSE, Environmental Science, Engineering, Occupational Health, or related fields.
	Minimum 2 years of experience in HSE management, safety compliance, or environmental management.
	Strong understanding of occupational health and safety principles.
	Ability to conduct inspections and audits.
	Strong reporting and documentation skills.
	Ability to work independently and enforce compliance standards. Professional
	HSE certifications are an added advantage.


Technical Skills: 


	Risk assessment and hazard identification
	Health and safety management
	Incident investigation
	Report writing
	Training and facilitation
	Communication and leadership
	Regulatory compliance.


Remuneration


	Competitive and based on experience.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their their CV and a cover letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position and location as the subject of the email.

Note


	Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
	PMN Farms is an equal opportunity employer committed to building a high-performance workforce and fostering professional growth across all its locations.
